title: podocarpus borneensis
intro: Podocarpus borneensis, also known as the Borneo plum pine, is a plant species that belongs to the family Podocarpaceae, characterized by its needle-like leaves and small, edible cones. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Podocarpus borneensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an anti-bacterial agent, making it a valuable herb in natural remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and cones are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
